2606.25984.title: InvestPhilBench: A Multi-Layer Dynamic Benchmark for Evaluating Large Language Model Procedural Reasoning in Expert Investment Philosophy
2606.25984.paper_summary: Large language models are increasingly deployed as investment research assistants, yet no benchmark tests whether they can accurately reconstruct and apply the specific procedural decision frameworks of expert investors. We introduce InvestPhilBench, a multi-layer dynamic benchmark spanning eight cognitive tiers, from principle identification (L1) to novel framework extrapolation (L8). The v0.6 release comprises 118 primary-source-verified investment principle cards, 25 decision framework cards with explicit topology metadata, and 243 QA questions (197 dev / 46 held-out test). For reproducible scoring at scale we introduce the Benchmark Automated Scoring Pipeline (BASP) -- five algorithmic metrics (OGRS, KCCS, SAP@k, IVP, CKCA) -- the Failure Mode Detection Protocol (FMDP) with computable rules for six failure modes, and Gate Reconstruction Accuracy (GRA), a per-gate metric for questions with gold reasoning programs. In this release, InvestPhilBench is primarily a benchmark-and-methodology contribution. A four-model sanity wave on the 188-question development split shows a sharp provider-tier split (BASP 0.906 vs. 0.438); these mixed-judge numbers are confounded upper bounds. The central finding: the BASP composite saturates at the frontier (Claude L4 = 0.932) while GRA still exposes a procedural deficit (frontier L4 GRA approx. 0.77, L7 GRA 0.57-0.62) -- composite scoring rewards fluent prose and hides the procedural gap. v0.6 implements a unified judge and true model-in-the-loop retrieval/oracle conditions; the de-confounded multi-model leaderboard and full three-condition run are v1.0 deliverables. On a 100-item expert-annotated gold set the automated BASP composite tracks the human reference at Pearson r = 0.72 (MAE = 0.10), with attribution (SAP@3) the weakest sub-metric and the failure-mode detector running sensitive-but-over-flagging.
2606.25984.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地被用作投資研究助手，但尚無基準測試其能否準確重建並應用專家投資者的特定程序決策框架。我們介紹了 InvestPhilBench，這是一個涵蓋八個認知層級的多層動態基準，從原則識別（L1）到新框架外推（L8）。v0.6 版本包含 118 張經過主要來源驗證的投資原則卡片、25 張帶有明確拓撲元數據的決策框架卡片，以及 243 個問答問題（197 開發 / 46 保留測試）。為了在大規模上實現可重複的評分，我們介紹了基準自動評分管道（BASP）——五個算法指標（OGRS、KCCS、SAP@k、IVP、CKCA）——失敗模式檢測協議（FMDP），具有六種失敗模式的可計算規則，以及門重建準確性（GRA），這是一個針對具有金標推理程序的問題的每個門指標。在這個版本中，InvestPhilBench 主要是一個基準和方法論的貢獻。對於 188 個問題的開發拆分進行的四模型理智波顯示出明顯的提供者層級分裂（BASP 0.906 對比 0.438）；這些混合評審的數字是混淆的上限。中心發現是：BASP 綜合指標在邊界處飽和（Claude L4 = 0.932），而 GRA 仍然暴露出程序缺陷（邊界 L4 GRA 約 0.77，L7 GRA 0.57-0.62）——綜合評分獎勵流暢的散文，並隱藏了程序差距。v0.6 實施了統一的評審和真實模型在循環檢索/神諭條件下；去混淆的多模型排行榜和完整的三條件運行是 v1.0 的交付成果。在一個 100 項專家註釋的金標集上，自動 BASP 綜合指標在 Pearson r = 0.72（MAE = 0.10）下追蹤人類參考，並且歸因（SAP@3）是最弱的子指標，而失敗模式檢測器運行敏感但過度標記。
2606.25984.author: Mingguang Chen et.al.
